name: taxonomy-datastore
summary: Full taxonomy-backed entity stack: model, adapter, datastore, registrar, and term-backed handler in one shot.
description: Creates a complete WordPress-backed datastore stack for a taxonomy: model, model adapter, datastore (interface + handler interface + implementation), the taxonomy registrar, and a term-backed handler that translates the typed datastore into wp_insert_term / WP_Term_Query / wp_update_term / wp_delete_term calls.
problem: You're starting a new entity that lives as WordPress terms and you want the entire idiomatic PHPNomad stack scaffolded in one shot, without copying boilerplate from another plugin.
appliesWhen: You're starting a new entity that will live in WordPress as a custom taxonomy
You want the conventional PHPNomad data layer end-to-end with no gaps
Consumers should access the entity through a typed datastore, not get_terms directly
The data is term-shaped (classification, hierarchy, named labels)
avoidWhen: You only need the taxonomy registered — use phpnomad/taxonomy
You already have a datastore — use phpnomad/taxonomy-handler
The data is genuinely relational — use phpnomad/database-datastore

general: full data layer for a taxonomy

examples: Set up everything for a regions taxonomy with typed CRUD
Scaffold a full data layer for topics backed by terms
tradeoffs: Generates seven files plus DI bindings and a Ready listener. Term meta_query is convenient but slow at scale. Use phpnomad/database-datastore if performance matters.
outputs: A {name}Datastore class plus its public and handler interfaces
A {name}TaxonomyDatastoreHandler implementing the handler interface against WordPress
DI bindings tying it all together plus a Ready listener for the registrar
postApply: Smoke test reads and writes through the datastore

objectType.description: Post type slug this taxonomy attaches to
